name: Collaboration in Open Research Projects
description: A comprehensive reference guide covering collaboration practices in open research — community setup, GitHub workflows, code review, meetings and events, leadership, and research infrastructure roles. Part of The Turing Way open handbook.
author: The Turing Way Team
learningObjectives:
After this lesson, the learner should be able to:
- Apply Git and GitHub for collaborative research and code sharing
- Demonstrate best practices for managing contributions in open science projects
- Identify strategies for fostering transparency and reproducibility in research outputs
- Implement collaborative workflows that support open science principles
- Recognize the importance of version control in research reproducibility
abstract: This lesson focuses on collaboration in open science and research projects, introducing best practices for working in teams, sharing code, and managing contributions using Git and GitHub.
audience: Researchers, Open Science Contributors, Software Developers
notes: Multi-chapter reference handbook. Linked URL is the guide landing page; sub-chapters cover community setup (~17 min), maintain-and-review (~10 min), meetings/events, leadership, and research infrastructure roles. Broad overlap with dedicated lessons in contributing and maintaining pathways — use as supplementary reading. timeRequired reflects one representative sub-chapter.
mentions: GitHub, https://github.com, A platform for hosting and sharing research code and projects
teaches: Collaborate on open science and research projects using distributed version control workflows.
